sequences.title: Telegram Redesign — Conversation-First
sequences.missionStatement: Replace the terminal-mirror/slash-command Telegram integration with a CLI-initiated conversation model. CLIs message the user via helm MCP; user replies are auto-injected into PTY stdin. Topic lifecycle syncs with session lifecycle. General Chat dashboard shows all sessions with Talk buttons.
sequences.sharedMemory:
## Key Decisions
- No terminal mirroring. CLIs call telegram_chat MCP tool to send messages.
- No slash commands. All UX via inline keyboard buttons.
- Every reply in a topic is always PTY-injected (no expectsResponse flag).
- Bot offline → telegram_chat returns { sent: false, reason } so CLI handles gracefully.
- Session removed → deleteForumTopic() in bot.ts (already exists).
- Topic deleted by user → clear topicId from session; recreated on next CLI chat.

## MCP Tools (3 total after consolidation)
- telegram_status
- telegram_chat (renamed from sendTelegramToUser, absorbs channel_create/send)
- telegram_channel_close

## Files being deleted
- src/telegram/terminal-mirror.ts
- src/telegram/commands.ts
- src/telegram/output-summarizer.ts
- src/telegram/reply-keyboard.ts
- src/telegram/text-input.ts
+ matching test files

## bot.ts already has deleteForumTopic() — reuse it, do not reimplement.


## Stage 1-3 Complete
- Stages 1-3 committed. Deleted old modules, added setup guide, rewrote RelayService.
- Key types changed: TelegramChannel no longer has expectsResponse. TelegramSendResult = { sent: boolean; reason?: string }.
- callback-handler.ts has stub params (any) for TextInputManager/OutputSummarizer — full rewire in Stage 7.
- orchestrator.ts has no-op stubs for feedPtyOutput/handleActivityChange etc — full rewire in Stage 7.
- helm-control-service.ts still has sendTelegramMessage (dead code, emits telegram:send) and createTelegramChannel/closeTelegramChannel — Stage 5 removes MCP tools.
- localhost-mcp-server.ts: telegram_set_output_mode dispatch returns no-op { mode: 'relay' }.
